mission.title: linkerd: Distributed Tracing Service Name should be dynamic
mission.description: Distributed Tracing Service Name should be dynamic. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current linkerd deployment
steps.description:
Verify your linkerd version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
helm list -n linkerd 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working linkerd installation.

steps.title: Review linkerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant linkerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl get configmap -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=linkerd
```
### What problem are you trying to solve?

As of today, the service name that the proxy uses to emit traces is hard coded to

steps.title: Apply the fix for Distributed Tracing Service Name should be dynamic
steps.description:
I did find a workaround that might be suited for most people.
If you're running the opentelemetry collector (which most probably are, because linkerd still uses opencensus), you can configure a custom processor that changes the `service.name` attribute.
The linkerd-proxy adds all pod labels as span
```yaml
transform/linkerd-proxy:
  error_mode: ignore
  trace_statements:
    - context: resource
      statements:
        - |
          set(
            attributes["service.name"],
            # TODO pick any labels you have/need here:
            Concat(
              [
                attributes["app.kubernetes.io/part-of"],
                attributes["app.kubernetes.io/name"]
              ],
              "-"
            )
          ) where attributes["service.name"] == "linkerd-proxy"
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl get events -n linkerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Distributed Tracing Service Name should be dynamic" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
I did find a workaround that might be suited for most people.
If you're running the opentelemetry collector (which most probably are, because linkerd still uses opencensus), you can configure a custom processor that changes the `service.name` attribute.

transform/linkerd-proxy:
  error_mode: ignore
  trace_statements:
    - context: resource
      statements:
        - |
          set(
            attributes["service.name"],
            # TODO pick any labels you have/need here:
            Concat(
              [
                attributes["app.kubernetes.io/part-of"],
                attributes["app.kubernetes.io/name"]
              ],
              "-"
            )
          ) where attributes["service.name"] == "linkerd-proxy"

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with linkerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
